triples.subject: Cox Automotive Dealer Sentiment Index (CADSI)
triples.sentence: The latest Cox Automotive Dealer Sentiment Index (CADSI) remained stable from Q1 to Q2 2024 despite dealer uncertainty in the market and economy.

triples.subject: Q2 current market index score
triples.object: most U.S. auto dealers see the market as weak
triples.sentence: The Q2 current market index score of 42 indicates most U.S. auto dealers see the market as weak.

triples.subject: used-vehicle sales index
triples.sentence: Conversely, the used-vehicle sales index remains under the 50 threshold, indicating a weak market.

triples.subject: Sentiment about electric vehicle (EV) sales
triples.sentence: Sentiment about electric vehicle (EV) sales fell to a new low in Q2.

triples.subject: majority of dealers
triples.object: existing tax credits as having a positive impact on EV sales
triples.sentence: a majority of dealers (with an index score of 57) see the existing tax credits as having a positive impact on EV sales.

triples.subject: Franchised dealers’ view of the economy
triples.sentence: Franchised dealers’ view of the economy, at 46, has been consistent for three consecutive quarters.

triples.subject: Cox Automotive
triples.sentence: Cox Automotive is a subsidiary of Cox Enterprises Inc., a privately owned, Atlanta-based company with $22 billion in annual revenue.
